hero.name: Anaëlle Mathé
hero.tagline: Crafting digital experiences with clean code and bold design.

about.title: About Me
about.bio: Software engineering student at Polytechnique Montréal, currently finishing my second year with distinction after a preparatory year and a baccalauréat mention très bien from Lycée Saint-Paul d'Angoulême. From July 2026, I'll be on exchange at the University of Technology Sydney.
about.bio2: What truly interests me is the intersection of technical rigor and creative sensibility. I believe great products are born from that balance — between clean code and design that makes sense.
about.bio3: Beyond code, I love art, the humanities, music, and dance. It's that mix that shapes how I approach problems: with curiosity, method, and a strong sense of aesthetics.

code.label: Clean code
code.desc: Readable, tested, maintainable — code is read more than written.

design.label: Thoughtful design
design.desc: Every pixel and interaction should have intent.

human.label: Human-first
human.desc: Tech serves people, not the other way around.

campuslift.title: CampusLift
campuslift.description: A student-centered crowdfunding platform where creators publish campus initiatives, collect micro-pledges, and engage through threaded discussions — with Stripe payments, live notifications, and content moderation.
campuslift.longDescription: CampusLift lets students fund campus initiatives through micro-pledges. Creators manage full project lifecycles (draft → published → archived), track funding in real time via Supabase, and receive email/in-app pledge notifications. Supporters browse by category, pledge via Stripe Checkout, and participate in threaded discussions. Built with Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, Supabase (PostgreSQL + real-time), Stripe, and Resend. 8 RLS-protected tables, 109+ tests, Playwright E2E, and a GitHub Actions CI pipeline.

placeholder1.title: University Project #1
placeholder1.description: A web application built as part of a university assignment, exploring full-stack concepts.

placeholder2.title: Embedded Systems Project
placeholder2.description: Programmed an autonomous robot to navigate a black-line circuit, avoid obstacles, and return to its starting position — using an ATmega324PA AVR microcontroller, C/C++, and state machine logic.

placeholder3.title: University Project #3
placeholder3.description: A data visualization tool built with React and D3.js for an academic dataset.
